summary: mnemonic-safety-check exfiltrates host information and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package `mnemonic-safety-check@1.6.1` ships an install-time and runtime exfiltration payload. `package.json` line 30 contains an inline Node script (loading `child_process`, `os`, and `https`) that POSTs hostname, working directory, and `process.env` data to `https://ddjidd564.github.io`. A sibling `scanner.js` file enumerates the filesystem (`fs.existsSync` checks against multiple paths), executes shell commands (`execSync`, `whoami`, `curl`), gathers OS metadata (`os.hostname()`), and transmits collected data to the same hardcoded `https://ddjidd564.github.io` endpoint via `https.request`/`https.get` POSTs. The package name suggests a wallet/seed-phrase utility, and the scanner's filesystem-walk + command execution + outbound POST pattern is consistent with a credential and host-fingerprint stealer. The destination is a GitHub Pages site under an unidentifiable user account (`ddjidd564`), not a publisher domain matching any legitimate maintainer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
